question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a monotonically increasing sequence check on transactionIndex in ContractResultRepository to cause a ContractResult with a very large transactionIndex to be inserted, making it appear as the last transaction in a block that actually has far fewer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractResult.getId() returns consensusTimestamp (a Long) to cause a hash collision in a HashMap or cache that uses the ID as a key, causing a different ContractResult to be returned for a given timestamp and reorganizing the cached transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a digital signature or MAC on ContractResult fields to cause a man-in-the-middle attack that modifies the consensusTimestamp of a ContractResult in transit, reorganizing the transaction history as seen by the client?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractResult stores transactionHash as a raw byte array without length validation to inject a truncated or extended hash value, causing hash-based lookups to fail and making the transaction unreachable by hash while still present in timestamp-ordered history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: findById(Long id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractResult stores bloom as a raw byte array with special handling for 256-byte all-zero values to inject a bloom filter that matches all log queries, causing the transaction to appear in every log-filtered history view and reorganizing the apparent sequence of log-emitting transactions?
